title: Vetiver and sea salt body scrub
subtitle: An exfoliating salt scrub with a deep, smoky fragrance
excerpt: A coarse sea salt scrub with sweet almond oil and vetiver and bergamot essential oils. The salt exfoliates; the oil conditions. Use in the shower on damp skin. Makes one large jar of scrub.
sourceNotes: Salt scrub formulation: standard anhydrous scrub base. Vetiver essential oil facial and body use safety: Tisserand and Young, Essential Oil Safety (2nd ed., 2014). Sea salt exfoliant properties: physical exfoliation via mechanical abrasion; no chemical AHA content.

recipe.batchNotes: Scale proportionally. Keep essential oil content at 2% of total formula weight.
recipe.makeAheadNotes: Ready to use immediately. Store with a dry spoon in the jar; introducing water contaminates the scrub.
recipe.shelfLifeNotes: Store in the sealed jar away from direct water contact. Use a dry spoon to scoop. The salt keeps indefinitely; the oil content gives a 6-month shelf life at room temperature. Discard if the oil smells rancid or if visible mould develops.

glossaryTerms.definition: A preparation containing no water phase. This scrub does not require a preservative as long as water is not introduced during storage or use.
Apply a small amount to the inside of the forearm and leave for 20 minutes before first use. Vetiver essential oil is generally well-tolerated on body skin at standard dilutions.

content.text: A salt body scrub is one of the simplest natural-home products to make: coarse salt as the exfoliant, a lightweight oil as the conditioning carrier, and essential oils for fragrance. Vetiver has a distinctive deep, smoky, almost earthy quality; bergamot balances it with a citrus brightness. At 2% of formula weight, both oils are within the standard body-product safe range. Sweet almond oil is the base carrier: it absorbs well enough that the skin does not feel oily after rinsing but provides enough lubrication during use to prevent the salt from scratching. This is an
preparation; no preservative is needed as long as the scrub is stored dry and used with a dry spoon.

items.prepNote: 75% of formula; medium or coarse grain; fine salt is too abrasive for body scrub
23% of formula by weight (oil is lighter than salt; measure by volume then weigh for accuracy)
1% of formula; deep, smoky base note
1% of formula; citrus brightness to balance vetiver

content.text: Weigh the sea salt into a mixing bowl. Add the sweet almond oil and stir to coat the salt evenly; the mixture should look like wet sand. Add the vetiver and bergamot essential oils and stir again until evenly distributed. Transfer to the glass jar and seal. The scrub is ready to use immediately.
on the inner forearm before first full use.

Use a dry spoon to take a small amount from the jar before getting into the shower. In the shower, apply to damp skin and massage in circular motions, concentrating on elbows, knees, and heels. Rinse thoroughly. Do not use on sunburned, broken, or irritated skin. Use once or twice a week; daily use is too frequent for the mechanical action of coarse salt.

Keep the jar dry between uses. Always use a dry spoon; water introduction shortens shelf life and can cause contamination. The oil content gives a 6-month shelf life. Discard if the oil smells rancid or if any visible mould appears.
